description: The provided image is unreadable and displays only a placeholder icon, so no visual scientific content can be directly verified. Based on the paper context and text extraction summary, this figure is inferred to correspond to the mechanical testing data for Fe-8Mn-5Al-0.2C low-density medium Mn steel. It likely presents engineering stress-strain curves, true stress-strain curves, and work hardening rate curves comparing the hot-rolled and quenched (HRQ) condition with samples intercritically annealed at 750°C for 5, 30, 60, 120, and 180 minutes. The extracted data indicate that the IA750_60 Min sample achieves the best combination of strength and ductility, with an ultimate tensile strength of approximately 658 MPa, total elongation of about 12%, and a peak product of strength and elongation (PSE) of roughly 7.9 GPa%. The work hardening rate reportedly exhibits two-stage behavior, with the IA60 condition showing the highest strain hardening capacity. These results support that the TRIP effect dominates the deformation behavior and that 60-minute intercritical annealing optimizes austenite stability and mechanical performance.
